name: Bangda Fry (Mackerel)
tagline: Konkani-style pan-fried mackerel — high omega-3, low cost

hero.alt: Two whole pan-fried mackerel coated in red Kashmiri masala on a banana leaf
hero.caption: bangda fry — masala-coated, shallow-fried

steps.title: Marinate the mackerel
steps.detail: Slit the mackerel diagonally on both sides for the masala to penetrate. Mix Kashmiri chili, turmeric, ginger-garlic, tamarind, and salt into a paste. Rub all over and inside the slits. Rest 15 minutes.
steps.tip: Tamarind both seasons and removes the fishy edge.

steps.title: Coat with rice flour
steps.detail: Right before frying, dust the marinated fish with rice flour on both sides — this gives the lacy crisp crust.
steps.tip: Coat just before frying — too early and it gets gummy.

steps.title: Pan-fry hot
steps.detail: Heat coconut oil in a wide pan till shimmering. Place the mackerel and cook on medium-high for 3 minutes per side without moving. Flip carefully, cook another 3 minutes. Drain on paper. Squeeze lemon and serve with rice.
steps.tip: Don't move the fish too early — the crust needs time to set or it'll stick.

healthFacts: Mackerel has 2.6g EPA+DHA per 100g — among the richest omega-3 sources.
Single fillet covers 100% of B12 and 90% of selenium daily needs.
Coenzyme Q10 content supports cardiac mitochondrial energy.
Lower mercury than larger predator fish — safer for frequent eating.
